    'software', 'computer', 'computers', 'processor', 'linux', 'microsoft', 'google', 'internet', 'web', 'server', 
    'servers', 'code', 'developer', 'developers', 'bug', 'debugger', 'stock', 'stocks', 'market', 'markets', 
    'economy', 'economic', 'election', 'elections', 'minister', 'parliament', 'iraq', 'war', 'bomb', 'terrorist', 
    'insurgent', 'troops', 'military', 'army', 'rebel', 'guerrilla'
}

def clean_and_tokenize(text):
    text = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9\s]', ' ', text.lower())
    return set(text.split())

def is_sports(title, desc):
    text = title + " " + desc
    tokens = clean_and_tokenize(text)
    
    if not tokens.isdisjoint(strict_veto_kws):
        return -100
        
    s_score = len(tokens.intersection(sports_kws))
    
    if 'olympic' in tokens or 'olympics' in tokens: s_score += 5
    if 'athens' in tokens: s_score += 3
    if 'medal' in tokens: s_score += 2
    
    return s_score

candidates = []
for a in articles:
    score = is_sports(a['title'], a['description'])
    if score >= 3:
        candidates.append((score, a))

if candidates:
    candidates.sort(key=lambda x: len(x[1]['description']), reverse=True)
    top_5 = []
    for s, a in candidates[:5]:
        top_5.append({"title": a['title'], "score": s, "len": len(a['description']), "desc_preview": a['description'][:50]})
    result = top_5
else:
    result = []

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(result))

var_function-call-10772560907913926086.description: AP - Michael Phelps, denied in his bid to win seven gold medals, picked up his second victory of the Athens Games by winning the 200-meter butterfly Tuesday night in Olympic-record time. Phelps fell just short of his own world record, holding off Takashi Yamamoto to win in 1 minute, 54.01 seconds. The hard-charging Japanese swimmer took silver in 1:54.56, while Great Britain's Stephen Parry won bronze in 1:55.22.
